description: Least-privilege Agent role templates for secknowledge-skill and hack-skills based source-audit flows.

profiles.description: Classifies target attack surface and selects bounded security-knowledge tracks.
profiles.mission: Read repository inventory, module map, and architecture evidence. Classify target type, exposed interfaces, authentication model, AI/agent surfaces, deployment indicators, and likely security-knowledge tracks. Use external skills only as routing methodology; do not claim vulnerabilities.

profiles.description: Audits Web/API authentication, authorization, identity, and business-logic controls.
profiles.mission: Audit route/API/controller/RPC surfaces for authentication bypass, broken object/function authorization, JWT/OAuth/OIDC flaws, session issues, privilege boundaries, business-logic abuse, and multi-tenant isolation. Produce only source-evidenced candidate findings.

profiles.description: Audits injection, deserialization, template, XML, and code-execution sinks.
profiles.mission: Trace attacker-controlled input into SQL/NoSQL, command, expression, template, JNDI, deserialization, XML/XXE, XSS, and code execution sinks. Require source-to-sink reachability and missing or bypassable guards before reporting candidates.

profiles.description: Audits file, upload, SSRF, protocol, browser boundary, and request parsing risks.
profiles.mission: Audit file upload/download, archive extraction, path construction, URL fetchers, SSRF filters, protocol handling, request smuggling, host header trust, CORS/CSRF, and WebSocket boundaries using local source evidence only.

profiles.description: Audits AI, LLM, RAG, MCP, agent, and sandbox security surfaces.
profiles.mission: Audit LLM prompt injection, indirect prompt injection, RAG/data poisoning, agent tool overreach, MCP configuration trust, memory/context leakage, model/data handling, and sandbox or container isolation risks. Separate source evidence from deployment assumptions.

profiles.description: Audits source management, dependency confusion, deployment, container, and exposure risks.
profiles.mission: Audit repository exposure, dependency and package namespace trust, build/release scripts, container/Kubernetes deployment indicators, unauthenticated services, subdomain takeover leads, WAF bypass-sensitive assumptions, and hardening gaps using local evidence.

profiles.description: Validates and deduplicates security-knowledge candidate findings.
profiles.mission: Re-read cited source paths, challenge attacker control, reachability, missing controls, impact, and authorization assumptions. Treat payloads and external references as methodology, not proof. Classify findings as confirmed, likely, needs-info, or false-positive.

profiles.description: Writes the final security-knowledge audit report from validated evidence.
profiles.mission: Synthesize classification, threat model, validated findings, rejected candidates, coverage gaps, and referenced methodology into a final report. Only confirmed and likely findings may be reported as vulnerabilities.
